summary:
Frontend-only verification of the new EDITABLE 'Toplam Borç / Gesamtschuld' feature in the expense-detail dialog on ProjeDetay. All 6 acceptance steps passed end-to-end against the preview URL (DE default UI). Login admin@arigastro.com/admin123 -> /. Navigated to /projeler, created project 'TEST_DEBTEDIT_ZZ' (customer=Esra Butik, amount=20000 EUR) -> project_id=616853c3-56a2-429b-80ad-6897932249f9. Opened project detail, added expense 'TEST_EXP_DEBT' with total_debt=15750 -> expense_id=68eb81a9-7498-458e-a87c-aa6cca2667f3. STEP-1: expense row correctly shows 'Bezahlt: €0,00 · Offen: €15.750,00 · 0 Zahlungen' and right-side total €15.750,00. STEP-2: Clicked expense-row-<id> -> [data-testid=expense-detail-dialog] opens. STEP-3: 3-card summary shows GESAMTSCHULD/BEZAHLT/OFFEN; the GESAMTSCHULD card renders as a clickable [data-testid=edit-debt-btn] with pencil icon showing €15.750,00 (verified btn text=€15.750,00, pencil svg present in DOM). STEP-4 CANCEL flow: clicked edit-debt-btn -> [data-testid=edit-debt-input] + [data-testid=save-debt-btn] + [data-testid=cancel-debt-btn] appeared; filled 99999, clicked cancel -> input collapsed back to display button with value UNCHANGED at €15.750,00; dialog does NOT contain '99.999' anywhere (confirmed no PUT was fired, editDebt state reset). STEP-5 SAVE flow: reopened edit, changed to 18000, clicked save-debt-btn -> success toast 'Ausgabe aktualisiert' (projects.expenseUpdated i18n key resolved to DE 'Ausgabe aktualisiert'). GESAMTSCHULD card now shows €18.000,00; OFFEN card recalculated to €18.000,00 (paid=0 so remaining = 18000, verified via 3-card innerText: ['GESAMTSCHULD
€18.000,00','BEZAHLT
€0,00','OFFEN
€18.000,00']). Underlying expense row in the list behind also updated to 'Bezahlt: €0,00 · Offen: €18.000,00 · 0 Zahlungen€18.000,00'. STEP-6 CLEANUP: closed dialog with Escape, went back to /projeler, clicked delete-project-<id> -> DELETE 200, row disappeared. ZERO console errors during the entire flow (only expected 401s from /me probe before login). Feature fully wired end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: ProjeDetay.jsx saveDebt() sends { total_debt: v } to PUT /projects/{id}/expenses/{expId} and reloads via load() -- correct pattern, keeps paid/remaining in sync with backend recomputation instead of trying to reconcile client-side.

editDebt state is explicitly reset (setEditDebt(false)) inside the expense-row click handler at line 232, so re-opening a different expense always starts in display (non-edit) mode. Good defensive UX.
debtValue is seeded from String(activeExp.total_debt) at click time -- if total_debt is an integer like 15750 the input shows '15750' (no formatting), which is the right behaviour for a number input.

Minor UX: saveDebt() validates v >= 0 but uses toast.error(t('projects.amountRequired')) as the fallback message for both NaN and negative values. A more specific 'invalid amount' key would be clearer, but not blocking.

The Cancel button correctly only calls setEditDebt(false) and does NOT reset debtValue -- so if the user reopens edit within the same session they see the previously typed (uncommitted) value. Verified this doesn't leak because the row-click handler resets editDebt to false and the next edit-debt-btn click reseeds debtValue from activeExp.total_debt. Safe.
The edit-debt-btn <button> uses type='button' (correct, prevents accidental form submit), inline-flex with hover:text-brand for affordance -- design consistent with the rest of the app.
Cosmetic: the pencil icon inside edit-debt-btn is 11px (very small) and text-zinc-400 -- adequate contrast but could be missed on first glance. Non-blocking.

Cosmetic: on the inline edit row (Input + Check + X buttons), all three elements are 7-unit tall (h-7) which fits nicely in the 3-card grid, but on very narrow viewports the Input may collapse below the Save/Cancel buttons. Non-blocking at 1600px+ viewports.

success_rate.backend: not tested (frontend-only per request; main agent already verified via curl 15750->18000)

seed_data_creation: Created 1 project 'TEST_DEBTEDIT_ZZ' (customer=Esra Butik, amount=20000 EUR) and 1 expense 'TEST_EXP_DEBT' inside it (total_debt=15750 -> 18000 after edit). Project + cascaded expense DELETED at the end of the run via delete-project-<id>. Preview DB is clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature testids on ProjeDetay expense-detail-dialog: [data-testid=expense-detail-dialog] (root), [data-testid=edit-debt-btn] (display state, button showing formatted total_debt + pencil icon), [data-testid=edit-debt-input] (number input in edit state), [data-testid=save-debt-btn] (Check icon, submits PUT), [data-testid=cancel-debt-btn] (X icon, only closes edit state). Login testids are login-email-input / login-password-input / login-submit-button (NOT login-email / login-password / login-submit). To create-project reliably at 1600x1200: eval_on_selector click for project-customer-select, then pick [data-testid=customer-option-<id>] with .click(force=True). Toasts appear as li[data-sonner-toast]; the update toast text in DE default is 'Ausgabe aktualisiert' (projects.expenseUpdated -> TR 'Gider güncellendi').
rca of the issue: N/A -- fresh-feature acceptance run. Implementation (frontend: editDebt/debtValue state + saveDebt() PUT + i18n key projects.expenseUpdated; backend: PUT /projects/{id}/expenses/{expense_id} accepts total_debt and recomputes paid/remaining) works end-to-end. No regressions on the surrounding expense CRUD (add-expense-btn, expense-row-<id>, delete-expense-<id>) or project CRUD (project-row-<id>, delete-project-<id>).
